name: fast-pauli
documentation:
Status
Usage
Package
Legal
--- ## Introduction Welcome to `fast-pauli` from [Qognitive](https://www.qognitive.io/), an open-source Python / C++ library for optimized operations on Pauli matrices and Pauli strings, inspired by [PauliComposer](https://arxiv.org/abs/2301.00560) paper. `fast-pauli` aims to provide a fast and efficient alternative to existing libraries for working with Pauli matrices and strings, with a focus on performance and usability. For example, `fast-pauli` provides optimized functions to apply Pauli strings and operators to a batch of states rather than just a single state vector. See our [benchmarks](https://qognitive-fast-pauli.readthedocs-hosted.com/en/latest/benchmarks.html) for more details about how `fast-pauli` can speed up certain functions compared to Qiskit. Our [Getting Started](https://qognitive-fast-pauli.readthedocs-hosted.com/en/latest/getting_started.html) guide offers an introduction to some of the core functionality in `fast-pauli`. ## Installation ### Pre-built Binaries ```bash pip install fast_pauli ``` ### From Source There are two strategies for building `fast_pauli` from source. One is a quick and easy method that uses all the default configuration settings. The other is a more configurable method that involves invoking `CMake`, `pip`, `pytest`, and other tools directly. #### Requirements - [CMake](https://pypi.org/project/cmake/) >= 3.25 - [Ninja](https://pypi.org/project/ninja/) >= 1.11 - C++ compiler with OpenMP and C++20 support ([LLVM](https://apt.llvm.org/) recommended) - [Python](https://www.python.org/downloads/) >= 3.10 - [scikit-build-core](https://pypi.org/project/scikit-build-core/) (ONLY for building from source with custom configuration) #### Build from Source (Linux) ```bash # Build python -m pip install -e ".[dev]" # Test pytest -v tests/fast_pauli ``` #### Build from Source (MacOS) ```bash # Setup python -m pip install --upgrade pip python -m pip install scikit-build-core brew install llvm # Build pip install -e . -C cmake.args="-DCMAKE_CXX_COMPILER=$(brew --prefix llvm)/bin/clang++;-DCMAKE_CXX_FLAGS='-stdlib=libc++ -fexperimental-library'" # Test pytest -v tests/fast_pauli # + other pytest flags ``` #### Build from Source (Custom Configuration) ```bash # Setup python -m pip install --upgrade pip python -m pip install scikit-build-core # Build python -m pip install --no-build-isolation -ve ".[dev]" -C cmake.args="-DCMAKE_CXX_COMPILER= + " # Test pytest -v tests/fast_pauli # + other pytest flags ```

functions.name: fast_pauli.qiskit_helpers.from_qiskit
documentation:
Convert Qiskit type to fast_pauli type.

Parameters
----------
qiskit_type : Any
    Qiskit type to convert to fast_pauli type.

Returns
-------
Any
    fast_pauli type.

Raises
------
NotImplementedError
    Conversion from Qiskit type to fast_pauli type isn't supported.

Returns
-------
Any
    Qiskit type.

Raises
------
NotImplementedError
    Conversion from fast_pauli type to Qiskit type isn't supported.

functions.name: fast_pauli.pypauli.helpers.pauli_matrices
documentation:
Provide a dictionary containing the Pauli matrices.

The Pauli matrices are a set of 2x2 matrices commonly used in quantum mechanics.
This function returns a dictionary with keys representing the matrix names
("I", "X", "Y", "Z", 0, 1, 2, 3) and values representing the corresponding matrix.

Returns
-------
    dict: A dictionary containing the Pauli matrices.

functions.name: fast_pauli.pypauli.helpers.naive_pauli_converter
documentation:
Convert Pauli string representation into corresponding dense matrix.

Parameters
----------
string : str
    The string representation of Pauli string.

Returns
-------
np.ndarray
    The matrix representation of the Pauli string.

functions.name: fast_pauli.pypauli.helpers.naive_pauli_operator
documentation:
Convert list of Pauli strings and coefficients into corresponding dense matrix.

Parameters
----------
coeffs : list[float]
    The list of coefficients corresponding to the Pauli strings.
strings : list[str]
    The list of string representations of Pauli strings.

Returns
-------
np.ndarray
    The matrix representation of the Pauli operator.

classes.name: fast_pauli.pypauli.pauli_op.PauliOp

methods.name: fast_pauli.pypauli.pauli_op.PauliOp.__init__
documentation:
Initialize the PauliOp object.

Args:
----
    coefficients: The input coefficients for the Pauli Operator
    strings: Set of Pauli Strings to compose an operator.

methods.name: fast_pauli.pypauli.pauli_op.PauliOp.pauli_strings_as_str
documentation: Get the list of Pauli strings.

methods.name: fast_pauli.pypauli.pauli_op.PauliOp.n_pauli_strings
documentation: Get the number of Pauli strings.

methods.name: fast_pauli.pypauli.pauli_op.PauliOp.copy
documentation: Return a copy of the Pauli operator.

methods.name: fast_pauli.pypauli.pauli_op.PauliOp.scale
documentation:
Scale each individual term by a factor.

Args:
----
    factors: The factors to scale Pauli operator's coefficients by.

methods.name: fast_pauli.pypauli.pauli_op.PauliOp.to_tensor
documentation: Return the dense matrix representation of the Pauli operator.

methods.name: fast_pauli.pypauli.pauli_op.PauliOp.apply
documentation:
Efficient multiplication of Pauli operator with a given state.

Args:
----
    state: The input state as a numpy array.

Returns
-------
    The result of multiplying the Pauli operator with the state.

methods.name: fast_pauli.pypauli.pauli_op.PauliOp.expectation_value
documentation:
Compute the expected value of the operator for the given state.

Args:
----
    state: The input state as a numpy array.

Returns
-------
    The expected value of the operator with the given state.

methods.name: fast_pauli.pypauli.pauli_op.PauliOp.__matmul__
documentation:
Matrix multiplication with other PauliOp or PauliString object.

Args:
----
    rhs: The Pauli operator or string

Returns
-------
    The result of multiplication

methods.name: fast_pauli.pypauli.pauli_op.PauliOp.__rmatmul__
documentation:
Matrix multiplication with PauliString on the left.

Args:
----
    lhs: The Pauli string to multiply with.

Returns
-------
    The result of multiplication

methods.name: fast_pauli.pypauli.pauli_op.PauliOp.__mul__
documentation:
Multiply the PauliOp with a scalar.

Args:
----
    rhs: The scalar to multiply with.

Returns
-------
    PauliOp: Pauli Op holding the product of the PauliOp and the scalar.

methods.name: fast_pauli.pypauli.pauli_op.PauliOp.__rmul__
documentation:
Multiply the PauliOp with a scalar.

Args:
----
    lhs: The scalar to multiply with.

Returns
-------
    PauliOp: Pauli Op holding the product of the PauliOp and the scalar.

methods.name: fast_pauli.pypauli.pauli_op.PauliOp.__imul__
documentation:
Multiply the PauliOp with a scalar.

Args:
----
    other: The scalar to multiply with.

methods.name: fast_pauli.pypauli.pauli_op.PauliOp.__add__
documentation:
Add two PauliOp objects together.

Args:
----
    rhs: The other PauliOp object to add.

Returns
-------
    PauliOp: Pauli Op holding the sum of the two PauliOps.

methods.name: fast_pauli.pypauli.pauli_op.PauliOp.__iadd__
documentation:
Add another PauliOp to the current one.

Args:
----
    rhs: The Pauli operator to be added.

methods.name: fast_pauli.pypauli.pauli_op.PauliOp.__sub__
documentation:
Subtract another PauliOp from the current one.

Args:
----
    rhs: The Pauli operator to be subtracted.

Returns
-------
    PauliOp: Pauli Op holding the difference of the two PauliOps.

methods.name: fast_pauli.pypauli.pauli_op.PauliOp.__isub__
documentation:
Subtract another PauliOp from the current one.

Args:
----
    rhs: The Pauli operator to be subtracted.

methods.name: fast_pauli.pypauli.pauli_op.PauliOp.extend
documentation:
Add another PauliOp to the current one
by extending the internal summation with new terms.

Note: this does a shallow copy of other's properties

Args:
----
    other: The Pauli operator to be added.
    multiplier: The coefficient to be applied to the new terms.
    dedupe: Flag to remove duplicate terms.
Class representing a Pauli operator with efficient operations.

modules.name: fast_pauli.pypauli.pauli_string
documentation: Efficient operations on Pauli string using numpy.

functions.name: fast_pauli.pypauli.pauli_string.compose_sparse_pauli
documentation:
Produce sparse representation of the pauli string.

Args:
----
    string: The input string representing the Pauli string.
    Must contain only I, X, Y, Z characters.

Returns
-------
    A tuple containing the column numbers and values of the sparse matrix.

functions.name: fast_pauli.pypauli.pauli_string.compose_sparse_diag_pauli
documentation:
Produce sparse representation of diagonal pauli string.

Args:
----
    string: A Pauli string containing only 'I' and 'Z' characters.

Returns
-------
    np.ndarray: diagonal values from resulting sparse matrix.

classes.name: fast_pauli.pypauli.pauli_string.PauliString

methods.name: fast_pauli.pypauli.pauli_string.PauliString.__init__
documentation:
Initialize the PauliString object.

Args:
----
    string: The input string representing the Pauli string.
    Must contain only I, X, Y, Z characters.

methods.name: fast_pauli.pypauli.pauli_string.PauliString.__str__
documentation:
Convert pauli string object to regular string.

Returns
-------
    str: Plain pauli string.

methods.name: fast_pauli.pypauli.pauli_string.PauliString.__repr__
documentation:
Get a string representation of the PauliString object.

Returns
-------
    str: A string representation of the PauliString object.

methods.name: fast_pauli.pypauli.pauli_string.PauliString.copy
documentation: Return a copy of the Pauli string.

methods.name: fast_pauli.pypauli.pauli_string.PauliString.to_tensor
documentation: Return the dense matrix representation of the Pauli string.

methods.name: fast_pauli.pypauli.pauli_string.PauliString.apply
documentation:
Efficient multiplication of Pauli string with a given state.

Args:
----
    state: The input state as a numpy array.
    coeff: Multiplication factor to apply to the PauliString

Returns
-------
    The result of multiplying the Pauli string with the state.

methods.name: fast_pauli.pypauli.pauli_string.PauliString.expectation_value
documentation:
Compute the expected value of Pauli string for a given state.

Args:
----
    state: The input state as a numpy array.
    coeff: Multiplication factor to apply to the PauliString

Returns
-------
    The expected value of the Pauli string with the state.

methods.name: fast_pauli.pypauli.pauli_string.PauliString.multiply
documentation:
Matrix multiplication of two Pauli strings.

Args:
----
    rhs: The other PauliString object to multiply with.

Returns
-------
    tuple containing the multiplication factor and resulting PauliString object.

methods.name: fast_pauli.pypauli.pauli_string.PauliString.__matmul__
documentation:
Matrix multiplication of two Pauli strings.

Args:
----
    rhs: The other PauliString object to multiply with.

Returns
-------
    tuple containing the multiplication factor and resulting PauliString object.

methods.name: fast_pauli.pypauli.pauli_string.PauliString.__add__
documentation:
Add two PauliString objects.

Args:
----
    rhs: The other PauliString object to add.

Returns
-------
    PauliOp: Pauli Op holding the sum of the two PauliStrings.

methods.name: fast_pauli.pypauli.pauli_string.PauliString.__sub__
documentation:
Subtract PauliString from the current one.

Args:
----
    rhs: The other PauliString object to subtract.

Returns
-------
    PauliOp: Pauli Op holding the difference of the two PauliStrings.

methods.name: fast_pauli.pypauli.pauli_string.PauliString.__eq__
documentation:
Check if two PauliString objects are equal.

Args:
----
    other: The other PauliString object to compare with.

Returns
-------
    bool: True if the PauliString objects are equal, False otherwise.

methods.name: fast_pauli.pypauli.pauli_string.PauliString.__ne__
documentation:
Check if two PauliString objects are not equal.

Args:
----
    other: The other PauliString object to compare with.

Returns
-------
    bool: True if the PauliString objects are not equal, False otherwise.

methods.name: fast_pauli.pypauli.pauli_string.PauliString.__hash__
documentation: Return the hash value of the PauliString object.
